tracks.meaning: to make a visible impression or line on a surface, typically with a pen, pencil, or similar implement
a symbol or character used in writing or printing, such as a letter or punctuation sign
a distinctive symbol, name, or logo used by a company to identify its products or services
a legally protected symbol, word, or phrase used to distinguish goods or services from competitors
a unit of type size in printing equal to one-seventy-second of an inch
a distinctive scar, spot, or blemish on the skin or body surface
a person of a specified kind or character, often used in a critical or derogatory sense
a significant sign, indication, or characteristic that reveals something about a person's nature or condition
a line or position that serves as a target or boundary in various athletic activities
a record of a person's criminal or disciplinary history
a specific point in time or moment when something is noted or recorded
a cue or signal indicating when an actor should begin speaking or performing
a piece of type bearing a character or symbol to be printed
a buoy or beacon used to indicate a navigational hazard or channel
a distinctive feature or characteristic used to identify a geological formation or stratum
a token or chip representing a fixed value in a game or betting activity
a specific target or objective for military operations or attack
